ORDER GRANTING AND DENYING FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT JOHN KITHAS NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SEPCIAL INTERROGATORIES AND FOR SANCTIONS - THE MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. FURTHER RESPONSES AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 17 AND 18 ARE ORDERED. THESE RESPONSES MUST COMPLY WITH CCP 2030.230. IF THE RESPONSE REFERS TO DOCUMENTS, SPECIFIC IDENTIFICATION AS TO WHERE THE INFORMATION CAN BE FOUND MUST BE PROVIDED. MOREOVER, THE ORIGINAL VERIFICATIONS FOR THE ORIGINAL RESPONSES AND THE AMENDED RESPONSES MUST BE PROVIDED AND ORIGINAL VERIFICATIONS FOR THE RESPONSES TO THE ABOVE ORDERED INTERROGATORIES MUST BE PROVIDED. ALL TO BE PRODUCED WITHIN 21 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. FURTHER RESPONSES AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 21 IS DENIED. THE INTERROGATORY MUST BE COMPLETE IN AND OF ITSELF. IT CANNOT REQUIRE REFERRAL TO OTHER DOCUMENTS TO COMPLETE THE RESPONSE. NO SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PRO TEM JUDGE: ELIZABETH RILES, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED =302/MJM
PANEL OF ARBITRATORS ASSIGNED. REJECTION DATE MAY-02-2013.
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED - STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER FILED BY DEFENDANT KITHAS, JOHN A.
AWARD FILED BY ARBITRATOR

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN KITHAS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE MOTION IS PREMATURE. THIS CASE WAS ORDERED TO ARBITRATION PURSUANT TO CCP 1141.11(C). AN ARBITRATION AWARD WAS FILED ON JULY 17, 2013. THE ARBITRATOR'S AWARD WILL BECOME FINAL "UNLESS A REQUEST FOR A DE NOVO TRIAL OR A REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL IN THE FORM REQUIRED BY THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL IS FILED WITHIN 60 DAYS AFTER THE DATE THE ARBITRATOR FILES THE AWARD WITH THE COURT." CCP 1141.20. TO DATE, NO PARTY HAS CHALLENGED THE ARBITRATOR'S AWARD BY REQUESTING A TRIAL DE NOVO. UNLESS AND UNTIL THAT OCCURS, THE MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS PREMATURE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nicolet E. Corliss, Esq. of Bradshaw & Associates, PC, Tel. 415.433.4800, for plaintiff and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT JOHN KITHAS' Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint is denied without prejudice. The motion is premature. This case was ordered to arbitration pursuant to CCP 1141.11(c). An arbitration award was filed on July 17, 2013. The arbitrator's award will become final "unless a request for a de novo trial or a request for dismissal in the form required by the Judicial Council is filed within 60 days after the date the arbitrator files the award with the court." CCP 1141.20. To date, no party has challenged the arbitrator's award by requesting a trial de novo. Unless and until that occurs, the motion for leave to file first amended cross-complaint is premature. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Sepcial Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. VANESSA K. WILLS OF THE BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 433-4800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOHN A. KITHAS, TEL. NO. 788-8100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JOHN KITHAS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Sepcial Interrogatories And For Sanctions - The motion is granted in part and denied in part. Further responses as to Special Interrogatories Nos. 17 and 18 are ordered. These responses must comply with CCP 2030.230. If the response refers to documents, specific identification as to where the information can be found must be provided. Moreover, the original verifications for the original responses and the Amended Responses must be provided and original verifications for the responses to the above ordered interrogatories must be provided. All to be produced within 21 days of this order. Further responses as to Special Interrogatory No. 21 is denied. The Interrogatory must be complete in and of itself. It cannot require referral to other documents to complete the response. No sanctions are awarded. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Pro Tem Judge: Elizabeth Riles, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported =302/MJM
